title: Propionylation and tryptic digestion
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8dlfh","text":"Protocol to derivatize (propionylation) and digest histone proteins into peptides with the purpose of bottom-up label free LC-MS/MS analysis. In this method, aspecific overpropionylation at serine (S), threonine (T) and tyrosine (Y) is reversed by adding hydroxylamine (HA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Start with vacuum dried sample (20 µg/sample)
Spin down & Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes
Spin down & Incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes
Add correct volume of 500 mM TEAB, CaCl2 and ACN
Add trypsin at a 1:20 ratio (w/w) => 1 µg trypsin/20 µg histones

Calculate the amount of 500 mM TEAB, Calciumchloride (CaCl2), acetonitrile (ACN) and trypsin necessary to result in a 1:20 ratio (w/w) (1 µg trypsin/20 µg histones) in a final volume of 50µL. Final conc CaCl2: 1mM Final conc ACN: 5%
Spin down & Incubate at room temperature for 30 minutes
Spin down & Incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes
Add 15 µL ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) at pH 12
Spin down & Incubate at room temperature for 20 minutes
Adjust pH with formic acid (FA): 30 µl 100% FA
Vacuum dry sample Store in -20°c or -80°C until further use
creator: Sigrid Verhelst
